items.label: Skill IR
items.target: 2.0 schema, root export, and target-neutral contract evidence
items.next_action: Keep IR as the source before target packaging.

items.label: Target Compiler
items.target: OpenAI, Claude, generic, Agent Skills compatible, and VS Code contracts generated from IR
items.next_action: Deepen target-native transforms when provider clients expose stronger runtime APIs.

items.label: Output Eval Lab
items.target: with-skill/baseline, assertions, execution evidence, blind A/B, failure taxonomy
items.next_action: Add more real-file and adversarial holdout cases as usage grows.

items.label: Provider Holdout
items.target: At least one real provider-backed holdout run with observed model/timing/token metadata
items.next_action: Run provider-backed holdout cases with real credentials and commit only aggregate evidence.

items.label: Human Adjudication
items.target: Real reviewer decisions recorded before claiming output review completion
items.next_action: Record real A/B choices in the decision template, then regenerate adjudication.

items.label: Benchmark Reproducibility
items.target: Public methodology, reproducible commands, required artifacts, and failure disclosure are machine-checkable
items.next_action: Keep the manifest current with every benchmark, package, and release evidence change.

items.label: Runtime Conformance
items.target: Target package structure, metadata, relative paths, and degradation notes pass
items.next_action: Keep target conformance fixtures updated as platform contracts change.

items.label: Trust Security
items.target: Secrets, scripts, dependencies, permissions, and package hash are reviewable
items.next_action: Keep high-permission approvals scoped, expiring, and target-mapped.

items.label: Permission Metadata
items.target: Packaged adapters expose explicit permission metadata and residual risks
items.next_action: Preserve residual-risk notes until real native enforcement exists.

items.label: Native Permission Enforcement
items.target: At least one target/client enforces approved permissions at runtime
items.next_action: Integrate a real client or installer runtime guard before claiming native permission enforcement.

items.label: Skill Atlas
items.target: Workspace catalog, route overlap, stale/owner gaps, drift, and no-route opportunities
items.next_action: Feed real drift data into Atlas once client telemetry is installed.

items.label: Registry Distribution
items.current: zip entries 521; install failures 0; permission failures 0
items.target: Package metadata, archive checksum, package verification, and install simulation pass
items.next_action: Regenerate registry after package verification so checksums stay aligned.

items.label: Review Studio
items.target: One page shows gates, evidence paths, blockers, warnings, actions, waivers, and annotations
items.next_action: Resolve human/external warning gates before claiming full release readiness.

items.label: Telemetry Drift
items.target: Local-first metadata-only event contract, aggregate drift report, hook recipes, and import path
items.next_action: Keep raw JSONL out of distributed packages and use aggregate reports for Atlas.

items.label: Native Client Telemetry
items.target: A real Browser/Chrome/provider client sends production metadata events
items.next_action: Install a real client against the native host and import production metadata-only events.
